column6: AI Dependency [Without AI support, I feel unsure about the quality of my academic work.]
column7: AI Dependency [Using AI makes me feel more relevant and competent in my studies.]
column8: AI Dependency [I rely on AI-generated suggestions to confirm whether my ideas are good enough.]
column9: AI Dependency [I worry that my performance will suffer if I donot use AI tools.]
column10: Alienation [AL1: Submitting AI-assisted work makes me feel less proud of my learning achievements]
column11: Alienation [AL2: I worry that AI-generated assignments donot truly reflect what I've learned.]
column12: Alienation [AL3: Using AI for coursework makes learning feel less intellectually rewarding.]
column13: Alienation [AL4: I develop fewer academic skills when I rely on AI to complete assignments.]
column14: Alienation [AL5: When AI can write essays or solve problems, I question why I need to learn these skills.]
column15: Alienation [AL6: AI capabilities make my own academic growth feel less valuable.]
column16: Alienation [AL7: AI tools reduce meaningful feedback from my teachers.]
column17: Alienation [AL8: Using AI for group work makes collaboration feel less authentic.]
column18: Communication [Comm1: I can clearly express my academic ideas in writing without relying on AI tools.]
column19: Communication [Comm2: I feel confident when explaining complex topics to others in class discussions or presentations.]
column20: Communication [Comm3: I adapt my communication style depending on the audience (e.g., peers, teachers, or formal reports).]
column21: Collaboration [Collab1: I prefer working alone with AI tools rather than discussing ideas with peers.]
column22: Collaboration [Collab2: I rely on AI to complete my share of group tasks instead of coordinating with my team.]
column23: Collaboration [Collab3: Using AI tools has made me less likely to seek help from classmates.]
column24: Creative Thinking [Creat1: I use AI tools even when I could think of original ideas myself.]
column25: Creative Thinking [Creat2: Relying on AI has made it harder for me to come up with creative solutions on my own.]
column26: Creative Thinking [Creat3: My assignments are less original because I adapt AI-generated ideas instead of developing my own.]
column27: Critical Thinking [Crit1: I rarely question AI-generated content even if it sounds suspicious.]
column28: Critical Thinking [Crit2: Since using AI tools, I tend to accept answers at face value rather than verify them.]
column29: Critical Thinking [Crit3: I find myself thinking less critically because AI often gives me quick answers.]
column30: Problem-Solving [Prob1: When facing a problem, I go to AI tools before trying to solve it myself.]
column31: Problem-Solving [Prob2: I have become less persistent in solving academic challenges because I know AI will help.]
column32: Problem-Solving [Prob3: I often skip brainstorming or planning because I expect AI to do most of the work.]
column33: Self-Confidence [SC1: I doubt my ability to complete tasks without AI support.]
column34: Self-Confidence [SC2: I rely on AI because I donot trust myself to get the right answers.]
column35: Self-Confidence [SC3: The more I use AI, the less confident I feel doing academic work on my own.]
